summary: The office is looking alive and polished. Vibe borders, nameplate pop animation, and role-colored lamps all landed nicely since #18. But the bones have recurring rot: WorkerCard memo is still getting smoked every 30s, the CSS monoliths keep growing (3198 lines, 120 keyframes), there is a reproducible SVG gradient ID collision when Luna goes idle, and two near-identical time-formatting functions live in separate files for no reason. Fun factor remains a solid 5. Score up slightly to 3.9 from 3.7, mostly for the visual polish ships.

suggestions.title: Luna SVG gradient ID collision when she goes idle
suggestions.description: CharacterAvatar for Luna creates a <linearGradient id="luna-hair-luna"> inside its SVG. When Luna is idle, she renders TWICE in the DOM simultaneously: once as a wandering Character (idleWorkers.map) and once as a mini-avatar inside ConferenceTable (meetingWorkers slice of idleWorkers, requires >=2 idle workers). Two SVG elements in the same HTML document with the same gradient ID means the url(#luna-hair-luna) paint reference resolves to the first DOM occurrence regardless of which SVG it is in. The mini avatar in the conference table will render with the wrong or missing gradient. Fix: make gradId unique per render by appending a uuid/size/context suffix, e.g. luna-hair-luna- or pass a unique instanceId prop to CharacterAvatar.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard memo defeat — stableActivityLog is a no-op useMemo
suggestions.description: This was flagged in review #18 and remains unfixed. The line  returns the identical reference as activityLog — it provides zero stability. Every 30s sync creates a new activityLog array, which invalidates the memo, which passes a new activityEntries prop to every WorkerCard, which defeats React.memo and re-renders ALL worker cards on every poll cycle even when the worker data itself is unchanged. Fix: either extract per-worker activity slices outside WorkerCard so only the relevant subset is passed as a prop (), or use a custom memo comparator on WorkerCard that ignores activityEntries reference changes when the filtered slice is identical. Remove the no-op stableActivityLog memo — it is dead code that misleads future developers.

suggestions.title: Hover card shows time-since-update, not time-on-task, for working workers
suggestions.description: The character hover card displays  with an hourglass icon, regardless of worker variant. For working workers, updated_at is the last data refresh timestamp, not when they started their task. So the hover card might show 2m when the worker has been on a task for 3 hours. The workers.json schema includes a startedAt field. Fix: for variant==='working', display formatIdleDuration(worker.startedAt || worker.updated_at) and label it clearly as 'on task'. For variant==='idle', keep updated_at with an 'idle for' label. Both labels should be explicit text, not just a clock emoji.

suggestions.title: formatIdleDuration (Office.jsx) and formatDuration (App.jsx) are identical — extract to utils
suggestions.description: Office.jsx defines formatIdleDuration(updatedAt) and App.jsx defines formatDuration(updatedAt). They are functionally identical: both compute elapsed time from a timestamp and return a human string like "3h 12m". App.formatDuration prepends "Working for" while Office.formatIdleDuration does not, but the core logic is duplicated. Fix: create src/utils/time.js with a single formatElapsed(timestamp) helper and an optional prefix param. Import from both files. This also prevents future drift where one gets a bug fix the other does not.

suggestions.title: completionToast and plantToast use inline style objects instead of CSS classes
suggestions.description: Both toast elements in App.jsx are rendered with large inline style={{ position: 'fixed', bottom: '2rem', ... }} objects containing 10+ properties. These are the only two UI elements in the entire codebase that bypass the CSS class system. This makes them impossible to theme via CSS variables, harder to animate consistently, and fragile if layout needs adjustment. The box-shadow even hardcodes rgba opacity inline. Fix: add .completion-toast and .plant-toast classes to App.css using existing CSS variables (--surface2, --text, etc.), move all the inline styles there, and reference the border-color via a CSS custom property passed as style={{'--toast-color': completionToast.color}}.

suggestions.title: No React ErrorBoundary — any render crash in Office.jsx takes down the entire app
suggestions.description: Office.jsx is 1263 lines of complex stateful rendering with SVG calculations, animation timers, and conditional logic. There is no ErrorBoundary anywhere in the component tree. If any render in Office or its children throws (e.g., a null dereference on worker data, a bad vibeKey, or an unexpected workers.json shape), the entire App unmounts and the user sees a blank screen. Fix: wrap the Office tabpanel and the dashboard tabpanel each in a lightweight ErrorBoundary that shows a friendly "Something went wrong in the office 🫠" message with a retry button. This is a class component wrapper (~20 lines) or use react-error-boundary package.

suggestions.title: Activity filter buttons use role="radio" on <button> elements — semantic mismatch
suggestions.description: The activity type filter bar and the worker filter bar both render <button role="radio" aria-checked={...}> elements inside a role="radiogroup" div. Applying role="radio" to a native <button> overrides its implicit button role but screen readers may announce the element inconsistently (e.g., NVDA announces "button" then ignores aria-checked). The keyboard contract for radio groups also differs from buttons — arrow keys should move between radio options, but these buttons only respond to click. Fix: either use actual <input type="radio"> elements styled as buttons, or keep <button> and drop role="radio"/aria-checked in favor of aria-pressed (toggle button semantics), which is what the interaction actually is.

suggestions.title: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS object literal defined inside Character component body
suggestions.description: The constant  is declared inside the Character function body at line ~365. Since Character is a memoized component that still re-renders on prop changes, this object is reconstructed on every render of every idle character. It should be extracted as a module-level constant outside the component. Tiny fix, zero risk.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline dots have no visual tooltip — accessibility-only hover data
suggestions.description: Each sparkline dot in the stats bar has an SVG <title> element containing the timestamp and vibe key. SVG titles are surfaced to screen readers and some browsers show a tooltip on hover, but behavior is inconsistent across browsers (Chrome does not show SVG title tooltips by default). Visual users hovering over the sparkline get no feedback. Fix: add a CSS-driven tooltip via the title attribute on a wrapper element or render a custom tooltip div on dot hover. Alternatively, add an aria-label to the entire sparkline like "Vibe history: crushing, in-flow, crushing" to at least make it useful for AT users regardless of the title bug.

suggestions.title: Footer version "v1.0.0-chaos" is a hardcoded string, not sourced from package.json
suggestions.description: The footer renders a hardcoded version string "v1.0.0-chaos" that has nothing to do with the package.json version field (which is currently "0.0.0"). Either both should be in sync or the footer version should pull from the build metadata. Fix: update package.json to a real semver (e.g. "1.0.0") and use Vite's define plugin to inject it:  in vite.config.js, then reference as  in the footer. The "-chaos" suffix can stay if you like the vibe.
